ground_truth_status.notes: All identified INS codes correctly match their declared functional classes (Raising Agents: 500i, 503; Emulsifiers: 442, 322; Food Colours: 160c, 110, 120, 129, 127, 132). flagged_codes.correct_functional_class: Acidity regulator, anticaking agent, raising agent, stabilizer, thickener

ground_truth_status.notes: INS 500 (Sodium carbonates) is not classified as a sequestrant by Codex; it is primarily an acidity regulator or raising agent. Additionally, INS 162 (Beetroot red) is declared under 'SYNTHETIC FOOD COLOUR', but it is actually a natural colour, which is a regulatory misclassification of origin, though its base functional class (Colour) is correct.

raw_label_text: REFINED WHEAT FLOUR (MAIDA) (60%), EDIBLE VEGETABLE OIL (PALMOLEIN, RICE BRAN), TAPIOCA STARCH, CORN FLOUR, EDIBLE COMMON SALT, DEHYDRATED VEGETABLE POWDER (ONION, GARLIC, TAMARIND), SPICES & CONDIMENTS 2% (DRY MANGO, CHILLI, PEPPER, CUMIN), SUGAR, YEAST EXTRACT, ACIDITY REGULATOR (INS 297, INS 261, INS 330), NATURAL & NATURE IDENTICAL FLAVOURING SUBSTANCES (MASALA), FLAVOUR ENHANCER (INS 635, INS 620), ANTICAKING AGENT (INS 554, INS 501(ii) (III)), PAPRIKA EXTRACT (INS 140).

ground_truth_status.notes: INS 501(ii) is a raising agent and acidity regulator, not an anticaking agent. Additionally, INS 140 corresponds to Chlorophylls (a green colour), which contradicts the declared ingredient 'Paprika Extract' (which should be INS 160c).
